summary: Found 2 new issues in `CartView.jsx`: the disabled waiter CTA logic is still inconsistent with the component's own unverified state handling, and the info help overlay is not exposed as an accessible dialog. The provided bundle only includes `CartView.jsx`, so AC-09's add-to-cart toast path could not be verified here.

findings.title: Waiter CTA only disables for literal `false`, not for the component's broader unverified state
findings.description: The submit button styling, disabled state, and helper copy all key off `isTableVerified === false`, but the rest of this component treats any non-`true` value as unverified (`isTableVerified === true ? ... : ...` in the verification block). Because this prop is not normalized locally, an `undefined`/`null` pre-verification state will still render the CTA as active-looking and clickable-looking even though the table is not verified yet.
findings.suggested_fix: Derive a single boolean such as `const canSubmitToWaiter = isTableVerified === true;` and use `!canSubmitToWaiter` consistently for the button classes, `disabled`, and the hint text.

findings.title: Info overlay is missing dialog semantics and focus management
findings.description: When `infoModal` opens, the help content is rendered as plain `<div>` containers with mouse handlers only. There is no `role="dialog"`, `aria-modal`, accessible name, or focus handoff into the overlay, so screen-reader and keyboard users remain on background content and may not discover the modal content reliably.
findings.suggested_fix: Use the app's dialog primitive, or add `role="dialog"`, `aria-modal="true"`, an `aria-labelledby` hook-up for the title, and move/restore focus on open/close.

missing_tests: Regression coverage for the waiter CTA with `isTableVerified` set to `undefined`, `null`, `false`, and `true`, asserting both styling and `disabled` stay aligned with verification state.
Accessibility test for the info overlay that asserts a `dialog` role with an accessible name is rendered and focus moves into the dialog when it opens.
notes: The review bundle's target code is `CartView.jsx`; the add-to-cart handler from `x.jsx`/menu item actions is not present, so AC-09 could not be verified from the provided artifacts.
I excluded issues already tracked in the bundle's Known Bugs section.
